_comment: Auto-generated by tools/build.mjs. Do not hand-edit. Run `npm run build`.

drafts.title: Cooking the Atlantic Crossing: The Galley Playbook
drafts.lede: Three weeks of provisioning, twenty crew, two thousand miles and a galley that pitches. An ocean crossing is won before you leave the dock — the freezer strategy, the rough-weather meals, and the morale that quietly runs on baking and snacks.

drafts.title: Cook the Port: Terroir as a Galley Workflow
drafts.lede: Most ports hand you one ingredient worth building the menu around. Cooking the place you are in — terroir as a workflow, not a romance — makes provisioning easier, the plate better, and the markup in Monaco optional.

drafts.title: Cooking for the Ozempic Guest
drafts.lede: More guests now board on a GLP-1 drug, and a rich tasting menu is exactly the wrong plan. The science of why appetite collapses on Ozempic — and the small, low-fat, protein-first service that keeps them comfortable, and fed.

drafts.title: The Equipment Verdict: What Survives a Season at Sea
drafts.lede: The galley runs on a handful of machines, and the fleet has hard opinions earned at sea. The gear that survives a season, the gear that dies on arrival day, and the one rule that keeps you cooking when a unit fails mid-charter.

drafts.title: Sourdough at Sea: The Science-First Loaf
drafts.lede: An open, blistered crumb and a deep mahogany crust — raised on a jar of flour and water you keep alive yourself. The wild loaf made reliable on a moving galley, where the acidity that flavours it also makes it keep. Interactive: scale it, tick it, cook it.

drafts.title: What Yacht Chefs Actually Earn: A 2026 Pay Map
drafts.lede: The honest 2026 yacht-chef salary map: base pay by vessel size, every package perk priced, the guests-aboard workload, the job ranked by demand, and the modifiers (solo vs teamed, private vs charter, experience) that set your number. Plus a build-your-number worksheet.

drafts.title: elBulli: The Coastline That Rewrote Cooking
drafts.lede: A beach bar named after two French bulldogs became the most influential kitchen of the last century — then closed at its peak to protect the one thing it was built for. The frontier elBulli explored, the chemistry it left behind, and what a galley can still steal from it.

drafts.title: Sea Urchin: The Candy of the Sea
drafts.lede: Uni is a reproductive organ that tastes of sugar — and the reason why is two amino acids, glycine and alanine. The full profile: the candy chemistry, the alum trick that hides age, the four ways to use it, and how to keep something this perishable alive on charter.

drafts.title: Koji: The Mould That Cooks For You
drafts.lede: One microbe turns rice into sweetness, protein into umami, and a tough cut into something tender — before a flame is ever lit. Meet the fungus Japan made its national organism.

drafts.title: The One-Hour Lunch: Market Meals That Feel Special on Provisioning Day
drafts.lede: Provisioning day, one hour to plate lunch, a galley full of boxes. The move isn’t to cook faster — it’s to let the market do the prep and assemble with intention. Here’s the playbook.

drafts.title: Signal Fire: One Chef, One Idea That Changes Everything
drafts.lede: Signal Fire: one chef, one idea that changes everything. Not a profile. A dissection of a single principle that survived decades in kitchens. Coming soon.

drafts.title: The Locker: Deep Pantry Guides for Chefs Who Stock Like an Arsenal
drafts.lede: The Locker: deep pantry guides for chefs who stock like an arsenal. Specific brands, specific reasons, three tiers. Coming soon.
